name: Visit to the Pyramids of the Italians - circular from Corconte
name_original: Visita a las Pirámides de los Italianos — circular desde Corconte
region: Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro traverse a landscape shaped by the Ebro Reservoir, offering varied terrain for outdoor exploration. The region features gentle hills and natural areas surrounding the water body, providing accessible paths. These trails often lead through open countryside and along the reservoir's edge. The area is designated for bird protection, indicating a rich natural environment. Best easy hiking trails in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro The most popular easy hiking route is Corconte – Pyramid of the Italians loop from Corconte, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Pisas Waterfall loop from San Cibrián, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail leads to a waterfall, providing a scenic natural feature. Local hikers also love the Church in Spain loop from La Población, a 2.6 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through local villages and countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro is defined by reservoir views, gentle hills, and natural bird habitats. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro?
A: There are 4 easy hiking trails specifically designed for a relaxed experience in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro. In total, the region offers 18 routes, catering to various difficulty levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails here?
A: The easy trails in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the reservoir views, gentle hills, and the natural bird habitats found along the paths.

Q: Are there any circular easy hikes around Embalse Del Ebro?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corconte – Pyramid of the Italians loop from Corconte&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.0-mile (6.5 km) circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church in Spain loop from La Población&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 2.6-mile (4.1 km) option.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of easy hikes in this area?
A: Easy hikes in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro typically range from 2.6 miles (4.1 km) to 4.0 miles (6.5 km) in length. Most can be completed within 1 to 2 hours, making them ideal for a leisurely outing.

Q: Are there easy trails that lead to waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find easy trails that feature waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pisas Waterfall loop from San Cibrián&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path that leads directly to a scenic waterfall.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these easy trails?
A: Many of the easy trails in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas designated for bird protection, to respect local wildlife.

Q: Are the easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. The easy trails, characterized by gentle hills and well-defined paths, are generally suitable for families with children. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corconte loop from Corconte&lt;/a&gt; offer shorter distances and manageable terrain for younger hikers.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on easy walks?
A: As the region is a 'Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves' (Special Protection Area for Birds), you can expect to see a variety of bird species. The natural environment around the Ebro Reservoir also supports other local wildlife, making for interesting observations during your walk.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints on the easy trails around Embalse Del Ebro?
A: Yes, many easy trails offer scenic viewpoints, particularly those that follow the reservoir's edge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corconte – Pyramid of the Italians loop from Corconte&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides excellent views of the reservoir and surrounding hills.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I find along the easy trails?
A: Along the easy trails, you can discover several points of interest. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5084696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain and Old Bridge at Embalse del Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7180507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Vicente de Villamezán&lt;/a&gt;, and various bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5541241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rutón Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Robla Train Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6907518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arija Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for easy hiking in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro?
A: The region is enjoyable for easy hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, but trails along the reservoir often provide refreshing breezes. Winter walks are also possible, offering a different, quieter perspective of the landscape.

Q: Are there parking facilities near the easy trailheads?
A: Yes, most trailheads for easy hikes in Zona De Especial Protección Para Las Aves Embalse Del Ebro, especially those starting from villages like Corconte or San Cibrián, offer convenient parking facilities for hikers.

Q: Are there options for winter walking on easy paths?
A: Yes, the easy paths around Embalse Del Ebro are generally suitable for winter walking, provided conditions are safe. The gentle terrain means less risk of challenging snow or ice, though it's always wise to check local weather and trail conditions before heading out.
tips: The Pyramid of the Italians is a mausoleum inaugurated on August 26, 1939 to house the corpses of the Italians who fell in the battle of Santander. It is located in the province of Burgos, a few meters from the border with Cantabria in the port of Escudo .

Corconte is a nucleus of the Cantabrian municipality of Campoo de Yuso. The town is known for its water, known as "Concorte water", which is bottled at the nearby Concorte spa.

It is one of the towns located on the shore of the Ebro reservoir, and is home to the Ebro Reservoir Interpretation Center.

Regarding its heritage, the church of Nuestra Señora de las Angustias stands out. The building is built of ashlar, from the 17th-18th centuries, with a baroque tower at the foot of the temple.

Also worth highlighting is the fountain and public laundry, located on the ground floor of the Council House. Originally the spring emerged in that same place, and hence its location, although currently the source is connected to the supply network. The washing basin has a rectangular plan and is built of ashlar.
